title: Grimms' Fairy Tales
author: Grimm, Jacob
summary: Grimms’ Fairy Tales collects traditional German folk stories translated into English, presenting magical adventures, tests of character, and moral lessons. Tales feature kings, peasants, clever animals, wicked witches, and brave youths who encounter enchanted objects, talking beasts, and perilous tasks. Recurrent motifs include golden treasures, lost children, cunning helpers, trials for marriage, and rescue from danger. Stories blend wonder and severity: rewards for kindness, punishments for greed or disobedience. The Golden Bird exemplifies these themes: three brothers, a talking fox, a princess, golden treasures, betrayal, and eventual justice. The anthology preserves folk motifs, archaic language, and stark moral contrasts faithfully.

characters.name: The King
characters.personality: authoritative, proud of his treasures, demands justice and rare items
characters.lifespan: remains ruler in the tale; commands quests and judges actions

characters.name: The Gardener
characters.personality: dutiful, anxious for his children’s safety, responsible for the garden
characters.lifespan: stays with his family; orders his sons to keep watch

characters.name: Eldest Son
characters.personality: ambitious but easily tempted and dismissive of counsel
characters.lifespan: fails quest, becomes lost/forgets home; later revealed to turn robber and is punished or arrested

characters.name: Second Son
characters.personality: similar to eldest, tempted by pleasure and merrymaking
characters.lifespan: fails quest, disappears for a time and later turns robber with brother

characters.name: Youngest Son
characters.personality: obstinate but kind, obedient to helpful counsel, humble and brave
characters.lifespan: succeeds through trials with fox's help, suffers betrayal by brothers but is ultimately rescued and restored

characters.name: The Fox
characters.personality: clever, patient, sacrificial, offers repeated counsel and aid
characters.lifespan: helps the youngest son through many tasks; requests to be killed at end but instead gives final counsel and departs

characters.name: The Princess
characters.personality: beautiful, emotional, prone to pleading but ultimately a prize to be protected
characters.lifespan: taken and reclaimed during the story; marries or departs with the hero depending on tale resolution

characters.name: The Groom
characters.personality: caretaker of the horse, asleep and negligent at critical moments
characters.lifespan: falls asleep and alerts guards when golden saddle is taken

characters.name: Soldiers / Guards
characters.lifespan: arrest trespassers and enforce the king's sentences

characters.name: King's Council
characters.personality: advisory body, values the golden feather and treasures
characters.lifespan: convene to assess the feather's worth and advise the king

characters.name: The Two Brothers (turned robbers)
characters.personality: dishonest, envious, treacherous toward their youngest brother
characters.lifespan: betray the youngest, steal his prizes, and are later unmasked or punished

characters.name: Villagers / People
characters.personality: practical, sometimes harsh (they demand ransom for prisoners)
characters.lifespan: participate as crowd, jurors, or executioners in the story's events
